heading: Expatriation to avoid tax
label: 877 Expatriation to avoid tax
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Revenue Procedures listed in a table under .section 1 of this title
Editorial Notes
References in TextThe date of the enactment of this subsection, referred to in subsec. (h), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 110–245June 17, 2008
Prior ProvisionsA prior section 877 was renumbered .section 878 of this title

Amendments2018—Subsec. (d)(4)(B)(i). substituted “in section 957” for “in 957”.Pub. L. 115–1412014—Subsec. (e)(2). struck out “subparagraph (A) or (B) of” after “event described in”.Pub. L. 113–2952008—Subsec. (e)(1). , amended par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Any long-term resident of the United States who—Pub. L. 110–245, § 301(c)(2)(A)“(A) ceases to be a lawful permanent resident of the United States (within the meaning of section 7701(b)(6)), or“(B) commences to be treated as a resident of a foreign country under the provisions of a tax treaty between the United States and the foreign country and who does not waive the benefits of such treaty applicable to residents of the foreign country,shall be treated for purposes of this section and sections 2107, 2501, and 6039G in the same manner as if such resident were a citizen of the United States who lost United States citizenship on the date of such cessation or commencement.”Subsec. (h). , added subsec. (h).Pub. L. 110–245, § 301(d)2005—Subsec. (g)(2)(C). substituted “section 7701(b)(3)(D)” for “section 7701(b)(3)(D)(ii)”.Pub. L. 109–1352004—Subsec. (a). , reenacted heading without change and amended text of subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (a) stated general rule on taxation of nonresident alien individuals who lost United States citizenship and provided that an individual would be treated as having a tax avoidance purpose if the average annual net income tax was greater than $100,000 or the net worth of the individual was $500,000 or more.Pub. L. 108–357, § 804(a)(1)Subsec. (c). , amended heading and text of subsec. (c) generally, substituting provisions setting forth exceptions for dual citizens and certain minors for provisions relating to inapplicability of presumption of tax avoidance to dual citizens, long-term foreign residents, minors who renounced citizenship upon reaching age of majority, and individuals specified in regulations.Pub. L. 108–357, § 804(a)(2)Subsec. (g). , added subsec. (g).Pub. L. 108–357, § 804(c)1997—Subsec. (d)(2)(B). , substituted “the 10-year period beginning on the date the individual loses United States citizenship” for “the 10-year period described in subsection (a)” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1602(g)(1)Subsec. (d)(2)(D). , inserted at end “In the case of any exchange occurring during such 5 years, any gain recognized under this subparagraph shall be recognized immediately after such loss of citizenship.”Pub. L. 105–34, § 1602(g)(2)Subsec. (d)(3). , inserted “and the period applicable under paragraph (2)” after “subsection (a)” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1602(g)(3)Subsec. (d)(4)(A). , struck out “during the 10-year period referred to in subsection (a),” before “any income or gain” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1602(g)(4)(C)Subsec. (d)(4)(A)(i). , inserted “during the 10-year period beginning on the date the individual loses United States citizenship” after “contributes property”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1602(g)(4)(A)Subsec. (d)(4)(A)(ii). , inserted “immediately before such contribution” after “from such property”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1602(g)(4)(B)Subsec. (e)(1). , substituted “6039G” for “6039F” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1602(h)(3)1996—Subsec. (a). , amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (a) read as follows:Pub. L. 104–191, § 511(a)“(a) .—Every nonresident alien individual who at any time after , and within the 10-year period immediately preceding the close of the taxable year lost United States citizenship, unless such loss did not have for one of its principal purposes the avoidance of taxes under this subtitle or subtitle B, shall be taxable for such taxable year in the manner provided in subsection (b) if the tax imposed pursuant to such subsection exceeds the tax which, without regard to this section, is imposed pursuant to section 871.”In GeneralMarch 8, 1965Subsec. (a)(1). , inserted “(after any reduction in such tax under the last sentence of such subsection)” after “such subsection”.Pub. L. 104–191, § 511(d)(2)Subsec. (b). , inserted at end “The tax imposed solely by reason of this section shall be reduced (but not below zero) by the amount of any income, war profits, and excess profits taxes (within the meaning of section 903) paid to any foreign country or possession of the United States on any income of the taxpayer on which tax is imposed solely by reason of this section.”Pub. L. 104–191, § 511(d)(1)substituted “section 1 or 55” for “section 1, 55, or 402(d)(1)”.Pub. L. 104–188Subsec. (b)(1). , substituted “subsection (d)” for “subsection (c)”.Pub. L. 104–191, § 511(b)(2)Subsec. (c). , added subsec. (c). Former subsec. (c) redesignated (d).Pub. L. 104–191, § 511(b)(1)Subsec. (d). , amended subsec. (d) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (d) read as follows:Pub. L. 104–191, § 511(c)“(d) .—For purposes of subsection (b), the following items of gross income shall be treated as income from sources within the United States:Special Rules of Source“(1) .—Gains on the sale or exchange of property (other than stock or debt obligations) located in the United States.Sale of property“(2) .—Gains on the sale or exchange of stock issued by a domestic corporation or debt obligations of United States persons or of the United States, a State or political subdivision thereof, or the District of Columbia.Stock or debt obligationsFor purposes of this section, gain on the sale or exchange of property which has a basis determined in whole or in part by reference to property described in paragraph (1) or (2) shall be treated as gain described in paragraph (1) or (2).”, redesignated subsec. (c) as (d) and struck out former subsec. (d) which read as follows:Pub. L. 104–191, § 511(b)(1)“(d) .—Subsection (a) shall not apply to a nonresident alien individual whose loss of United States citizenship resulted from the application of section 301(b), 350, or 355 of the Immigration and Nationality Act, as amended (, 1482, or 1487).”Exception for Loss of Citizenship for Certain Causes8 U.S.C. 1401(b)Subsecs. (e), (f). , added subsec. (e) and redesignated former subsec. (e) as (f).Pub. L. 104–191, § 511(f)(1)1992—Subsec. (b). substituted “402(d)(1)” for “402(e)(1)”.Pub. L. 102–3181986—Subsec. (c). inserted at end “For purposes of this section, gain on the sale or exchange of property which has a basis determined in whole or in part by reference to property described in paragraph (1) or (2) shall be treated as gain described in paragraph (1) or (2).”Pub. L. 99–5141980—Subsec. (b). substituted “55, or 402(e)(1)” for “section 55, 402(e)(1), or section 1201(b)”.Pub. L. 96–2221978—Subsec. (b). substituted “section 1, section 55,” for “section 1”.Pub. L. 95–6001976—Subsecs. (b)(2), (e). struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary”.Pub. L. 94–4551974—Subsec. (b). inserted reference to section 402(e)(1).Pub. L. 93–406

L. 104–191, title V, § 511(g)110 Stat. 2100Aug. 21, 1996“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 2107 and 2501 of this title] shall apply to—“(A)individuals losing United States citizenship (within the meaning of section 877 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986) on or after , andFebruary 6, 1995“(B)long-term residents of the United States with respect to whom an event described in [former] subparagraph (A) or (B) of section 877(e)(1) of such Code occurs on or after .February 6, 1995“(2).—Ruling requestsIn no event shall the 1-year period referred to in section 877(c)(1)(B) of such Code, as amended by this section, expire before the date which is 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [].Aug. 21, 1996“(3)Special rule.—“(A).—In generalIn the case of an individual who performed an act of expatriation specified in paragraph (1), (2), (3), or (4) of section 349(a) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (–(4)) before , but who did not, on or before such date, furnish to the United States Department of State a signed statement of voluntary relinquishment of United States nationality confirming the performance of such act, the amendments made by this section and section 512 [enacting ] shall apply to such individual except that the 10-year period described in section 877(a) of such Code shall not expire before the end of the 10-year period beginning on the date such statement is so furnished.8 U.S.C. 1481(a)(1)section 6039F of this titleFebruary 6, 1995“(B).—ExceptionSubparagraph (A) shall not apply if the individual establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary of the Treasury that such loss of United States citizenship occurred before .”February 6, 1994Amendment by applicable to taxable years beginning after , with retention of certain transition rules, see , set out as a note under .Pub. children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Every nonresident alien individual to whom this section applies and who, within the 10-year period immediately preceding the close of the taxable year, lost United States citizenship shall be taxable for such taxable year in the manner provided in subsection (b) if the tax imposed pursuant to such subsection (after any reduction in such tax under the last sentence of such subsection) exceeds the tax which, without regard to this section, is imposed pursuant to section 871.

children.label: A
children.text: the average annual net income tax (as defined in section 38(c)(1)) of such individual for the period of 5 taxable years ending before the date of the loss of United States citizenship is greater than $124,000,

children.label: B
children.text: the net worth of the individual as of such date is $2,000,000 or more, or

children.label: C
children.text: such individual fails to certify under penalty of perjury that he has met the requirements of this title for the 5 preceding taxable years or fails to submit such evidence of such compliance as the Secretary may require.
the gross income shall include only the gross income described in section 872(a) (as modified by subsection (d) of this section), and

the deductions shall be allowed if and to the extent that they are connected with the gross income included under this section, except that the capital loss carryover provided by section 1212(b) shall not be allowed; and the proper allocation and apportionment of the deductions for this purpose shall be determined as provided under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (a)(2) shall not apply to an individual described in paragraph (2) or (3).

children.label: i
children.text: the individual became at birth a citizen of the United States and a citizen of another country and continues to be a citizen of such other country, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the individual has had no substantial contacts with the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: was never a resident of the United States (as defined in section 7701(b)),

children.label: ii
children.text: has never held a United States passport, and

children.label: iii
children.text: was not present in the United States for more than 30 days during any calendar year which is 1 of the 10 calendar years preceding the individual’s loss of United States citizenship.

children.label: A
children.text: the individual became at birth a citizen of the United States,

children.label: B
children.text: neither parent of such individual was a citizen of the United States at the time of such birth,

children.label: C
children.text: the individual’s loss of United States citizenship occurs before such individual attains age 18½, and

children.label: D
children.text: the individual was not present in the United States for more than 30 days during any calendar year which is 1 of the 10 calendar years preceding the individual’s loss of United States citizenship.

children.heading: Special rules for source, etc.
children.label: d Special rules for source, etc.

children.heading: Sale of property
children.label: A Sale of property
children.text: Gains on the sale or exchange of property (other than stock or debt obligations) located in the United States.

children.heading: Stock or debt obligations
children.label: B Stock or debt obligations
children.text: Gains on the sale or exchange of stock issued by a domestic corporation or debt obligations of United States persons or of the United States, a State or political subdivision thereof, or the District of Columbia.

children.heading: Income or gain derived from controlled foreign corporation
children.label: C Income or gain derived from controlled foreign corporation
I
children.text: the total combined voting power of all classes of stock entitled to vote of such corporation, or

children.label: II
children.text: the total value of the stock of such corporation, and

children.label: ii
children.text: to the extent such income or gain does not exceed the earnings and profits attributable to such stock which were earned or accumulated before the loss of citizenship and during periods that the ownership requirements of clause (i) are met.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any exchange of property to which this paragraph applies, notwithstanding any other provision of this title, such property shall be treated as sold for its fair market value on the date of such exchange, and any gain shall be recognized for the taxable year which includes such date.

children.heading: Exchanges to which paragraph applies
children.label: B Exchanges to which paragraph applies
i
children.text: gain would not (but for this paragraph) be recognized on such exchange in whole or in part for purposes of this subtitle,

children.label: ii
children.text: income derived from such property was from sources within the United States (or, if no income was so derived, would have been from such sources), and

children.label: iii
children.text: income derived from the property acquired in the exchange would be from sources outside the United States.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: C Exception
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply if the individual enters into an agreement with the Secretary which specifies that any income or gain derived from the property acquired in the exchange (or any other property which has a basis determined in whole or part by reference to such property) during such 10-year period shall be treated as from sources within the United States. If the property transferred in the exchange is disposed of by the person acquiring such property, such agreement shall terminate and any gain which was not recognized by reason of such agreement shall be recognized as of the date of such disposition.

children.heading: Secretary may extend period
children.label: D Secretary may extend period
children.text: To the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, subparagraph (B) shall be applied by substituting the 15-year period beginning 5 years before the loss of United States citizenship for the 10-year period referred to therein. In the case of any exchange occurring during such 5 years, any gain recognized under this subparagraph shall be recognized immediately after such loss of citizenship.

children.heading: Secretary may require recognition of gain in certain cases
children.label: E Secretary may require recognition of gain in certain cases
i
children.text: the removal of appreciated tangible personal property from the United States, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any other occurrence which (without recognition of gain) results in a change in the source of the income or gain from property from sources within the United States to sources outside the United States,

children.heading: Substantial diminishing of risks of ownership
children.label: 3 Substantial diminishing of risks of ownership
A
children.text: the holding of a put with respect to such property (or similar property),

children.label: B
children.text: the holding by another person of a right to acquire the property, or

children.label: C
children.text: a short sale or any other transaction.

children.heading: Treatment of property contributed to controlled foreign corporations
children.label: 4 Treatment of property contributed to controlled foreign corporations
i
children.text: an individual losing United States citizenship contributes property during the 10-year period beginning on the date the individual loses United States citizenship to any corporation which, at the time of the contribution, is described in subparagraph (B), and

children.label: ii
children.text: income derived from such property immediately before such contribution was from sources within the United States (or, if no income was so derived, would have been from such sources),

children.label: i
children.text: such corporation would be a controlled foreign corporation (as defined in section 957), and

children.label: ii
children.text: such individual would be a United States shareholder (as defined in section 951(b)) with respect to such corporation.

children.heading: Disposition of stock in corporation
children.label: C Disposition of stock in corporation
children.text: If stock in the corporation referred to in subparagraph (A) (or any other stock which has a basis determined in whole or part by reference to such stock) is disposed of during the 10-year period referred to in subsection (a) and while the property referred to in subparagraph (A) is held by such corporation, a pro rata share of such property (determined on the basis of the value of such stock) shall be treated as sold by the corporation immediately before such disposition.

children.label: i
children.text: the property is sold to the corporation, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the property taken into account under subparagraph (A) is sold by the corporation.

children.heading: Information reporting
children.label: E Information reporting
children.text: The Secretary shall require such information reporting as is necessary to carry out the purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Comparable treatment of lawful permanent residents who cease to be taxed as residents
children.label: e Comparable treatment of lawful permanent residents who cease to be taxed as residents

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any long-term resident of the United States who ceases to be a lawful permanent resident of the United States (within the meaning of section 7701(b)(6)) shall be treated for purposes of this section and sections 2107, 2501, and 6039G in the same manner as if such resident were a citizen of the United States who lost United States citizenship on the date of such cessation or commencement.

children.heading: Long-term resident
children.label: 2 Long-term resident
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “long-term resident” means any individual (other than a citizen of the United States) who is a lawful permanent resident of the United States in at least 8 taxable years during the period of 15 taxable years ending with the taxable year during which the event described in paragraph (1) occurs. For purposes of the preceding sentence, an individual shall not be treated as a lawful permanent resident for any taxable year if such individual is treated as a resident of a foreign country for the taxable year under the provisions of a tax treaty between the United States and the foreign country and does not waive the benefits of such treaty applicable to residents of the foreign country.

children.heading: Exceptions not to apply
children.label: A Exceptions not to apply
children.text: Subsection (c) shall not apply to an individual who is treated as provided in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Step-up in basis
children.label: B Step-up in basis
children.text: Solely for purposes of determining any tax imposed by reason of this subsection, property which was held by the long-term resident on the date the individual first became a resident of the United States shall be treated as having a basis on such date of not less than the fair market value of such property on such date. The preceding sentence shall not apply if the individual elects not to have such sentence apply. Such an election, once made, shall be irrevocable.

children.heading: Authority to exempt individuals
children.label: 4 Authority to exempt individuals
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to an individual who is described in a category of individuals prescribed by regulation by the Secretary.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 5 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be appropriate to carry out this subsection, including regulations providing for the application of this subsection in cases where an alien individual becomes a resident of the United States during the 10-year period after being treated as provided in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Burden of proof
children.label: f Burden of proof
children.text: If the Secretary establishes that it is reasonable to believe that an individual’s loss of United States citizenship would, but for this section, result in a substantial reduction for the taxable year in the taxes on his probable income for such year, the burden of proving for such taxable year that such loss of citizenship did not have for one of its principal purposes the avoidance of taxes under this subtitle or subtitle B shall be on such individual.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: This section shall not apply to any individual to whom this section would otherwise apply for any taxable year during the 10-year period referred to in subsection (a) in which such individual is physically present in the United States at any time on more than 30 days in the calendar year ending in such taxable year, and such individual shall be treated for purposes of this title as a citizen or resident of the United States, as the case may be, for such taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: such employer is related (within the meaning of section 267 and 707) to such individual, or

children.label: ii
children.text: such employer fails to meet such requirements as the Secretary may prescribe by regulations to prevent the avoidance of the purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Individuals with ties to other countries
children.label: B Individuals with ties to other countries
I
children.text: such individual was born,

children.label: II
children.text: if such individual is married, such individual’s spouse was born, or

children.label: III
children.text: either of such individual’s parents were born, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the individual becomes fully liable for income tax in such country.

children.heading: Minimal prior physical presence in the United States
children.label: C Minimal prior physical presence in the United States
children.text: An individual is described in this subparagraph if, for each year in the 10-year period ending on the date of loss of United States citizenship or termination of residency, the individual was physically present in the United States for 30 days or less. The rule of section 7701(b)(3)(D) shall apply for purposes of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: h Termination
children.text: This section shall not apply to any individual whose expatriation date (as defined in section 877A(g)(3)) is on or after the date of the enactment of this subsection.
